hawkish_phrases: further increases in the federal funds rate
operating at or near maximum employment
stronger spending by businesses and households as a result of improved sentiment
a more rapid buildup of inflationary pressures than anticipated
could warrant a faster pace of scaling back accommodation
dovish_phrases: core inflation was little changed and continued to run somewhat below 2 percent
gradual removal of policy accommodation likely would be appropriate
the federal funds rate is likely to remain, for some time, below levels that are expected to prevail in the longer run
risk-management considerations still argued for a gradual removal of accommodation
it could take some time for inflation to rise to 2 percent on a sustained basis
summary: The FOMC raised the target range by 25bp to 3/4 to 1 percent with one dissent (Kashkari preferring to hold), emphasizing gradual future increases while noting the economy is at or near maximum employment and inflation has moved close to 2 percent, though core inflation remains somewhat below target.
